message:
feat(fuzz): add fuzz_fortify + file bd-ye0f4 (bd-g2bd5 regression)

## FULL REVIEW MODE findings

- cargo test --workspace --all-targets (default parallelism) still
  SIGABRTs with `free(): invalid size` in glibc_internal_abi_test,
  even after the a581f0ac TLS dtor isolation fix. Filed as bd-ye0f4
  [CRITICAL] regression against bd-g2bd5. The race has moved (doesn't
  reproduce on isolated runs anymore, only under workspace parallel
  test), which is progress but not a fix.
- bd-nnjiz (elimination flake) also got fixed upstream this session
  via 901b7bcc. Confirmed by the clean --test-threads=1 workspace
  pass after the threshold normalization.
- cargo test --workspace -- --test-threads=1 passes every suite.
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean.

## Fuzz gap drain — bd-dvr22 priority-4

crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_fortify.rs covers the
FORTIFY_SOURCE `__*_chk` safe path: __memcpy_chk, __memmove_chk,
__memset_chk, __explicit_bzero_chk, __strcpy_chk, __strncpy_chk,
__strcat_chk, __strncat_chk, __stpcpy_chk, __stpncpy_chk,
__snprintf_chk.

Design constraint: `__chk_fail()` is `-> !` and terminates the
process via abort. We cannot fuzz the overflow-trip path in-process
(libFuzzer's persistent mode would die on first overflow). This
harness therefore exercises the **safe path**:

- Clamps fuzzer inputs so len ≤ destlen (overflow check is not
  tripped).
- Exercises destlen == usize::MAX (the "I don't have destlen"
  escape hatch) so the overflow branch is skipped regardless of len.
- Oracle: _chk output equals non-_chk counterpart byte-for-byte on
  the safe path. A direct divergence here means FORTIFY_SOURCE is
  silently mis-copying.
- Guard sentinels (0xFD × 64) on both sides of dst so off-by-one
  inside the _chk wrapper itself is detected.
- __explicit_bzero_chk: explicit check that bytes 0..len are 0 and
  bytes len..dst_size are untouched.
- __stpcpy_chk: verifies the returned end-of-string pointer has the
  correct offset from dst.

Overflow-path coverage (the __chk_fail abort) requires a fork-based
harness and is tracked as follow-up in bd-dvr22 fortify notes.

Validation: cargo build --bin fuzz_fortify clean (6.1s); cargo
clippy --bin fuzz_fortify --no-deps -- -D warnings clean after one
enum_variant_names allow (all ops legitimately share the _Chk
suffix — that IS the interesting distinction from non-_chk).

Continues bd-dvr22 drain: mmap → signal → socket → fortify.
Remaining: setjmp, dlfcn, pthread (TSan), c11threads (TSan).

Agent: cc-libc
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -667,7 +667,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-geoa","title":"resolv: getaddrinfo_a zero-item and null-request-list degenerate calls should succeed","description":"Host glibc returns success with errno unchanged for synchronous-degenerate getaddrinfo_a calls such as nitems=0 (even with a NULL list) and positive-length request arrays whose entries are all NULL. FrankenLibC currently returns EAI_SYSTEM/ENOSYS. Restore those no-op success semantics in unistd_abi.rs and lock them with focused ABI tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:27:52.529653096Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T06:30:33.157717821Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T06:30:33.155545909Z","close_reason":"Restored host-parity getaddrinfo_a success semantics for zero-item and all-NULL synchronous-degenerate request sets, with focused ABI regression coverage verified locally and via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-gh8m4","title":"syscall: sched priority bounds for SCHED_DEADLINE should be zero exactly","description":"Host glibc returns min=0 and max=0 for sched_get_priority_{min,max}(SCHED_DEADLINE). Add focused ABI regression coverage so the Linux deadline-policy bounds contract is frozen exactly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:33:47.405198256Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:42:33.365913964Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T04:42:33.363277112Z","close_reason":"Added sched_priority_bounds_sched_deadline_are_zero asserting min=max=0 host-parity for SCHED_DEADLINE (policy 6).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-gii3","title":"raise(0) incorrectly rejects POSIX null signal","description":"Our frankenlibc_abi::signal_abi::raise() checks valid_signal(sig) which requires sig in 1..=64. POSIX specifies raise(0) must succeed as the 'null signal' (no-op sending, checks tgkill permissions only). Host glibc's raise(0) does tgkill(pid,tid,0) which succeeds. Impacts gsignal(0) via raise(). Fix: add explicit null-signal handling to signal_abi::raise() before valid_signal check, calling tgkill(pid,tid,0) directly. Discovered while expanding signal_ops conformance fixtures (bd-9n4k).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T17:45:39.193198411Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T17:56:13.339368276Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T17:56:13.337394740Z","close_reason":"Implemented POSIX null-signal raise/gsignal semantics","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-gilq3","title":"[HIGH] bd15n2_fixture_gap_fill_artifacts_test fails: 'could not locate libfrankenlibc_abi.so'","description":"## Symptom

`cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test bd15n2_fixture_gap_fill_artifacts_test` fails:

```
thread 'fixture_gap_fill_gate_emits_valid_bd15n2_artifacts' panicked at
crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/bd15n2_fixture_gap_fill_artifacts_test.rs:42:5:
gate script failed:
stdout=
stderr=    Finished `release` profile [optimized] target(s) in 0.06s
FAIL: could not locate libfrankenlibc_abi.so
```

## Diagnosis

The test at line 42 invokes a gate script (presumably under scripts/) that needs the built `libfrankenlibc_abi.so` cdylib. The script builds a release profile of frankenlibc-abi (that step succeeds in the above log), then fails to *find* the resulting .so at the path it expects. The stderr only shows the cargo 'Finished release' line, no locator stderr from the script — the script likely greps `target/release` for the .so but we're running under `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc` which the script doesn't respect.

## Fix direction

1. Read crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/bd15n2_fixture_gap_fill_artifacts_test.rs:42 and trace which gate script it spawns.
2. That script likely hardcodes `target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so`; change it to honour `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` (or at least look at `/data/tmp/cargo-target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so` when set) or fall back to `cargo metadata --format-version=1 | jq -r .target_directory`/`release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so`.

## Blast radius

Fails even in-isolation — test is always broken under a non-default `CARGO_TARGET_DIR`. Blocks the bd15n2 fixture-gap-fill gate from being exercised in any CI environment that redirects target_dir (rch, sccache, CI caches, etc.).

## Verification

- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test bd15n2_fixture_gap_fill_artifacts_test` — reproduces reliably with `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc`.
- Expected to pass under default `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` (unset → defaults to `target/`).
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T13:18:06.994113107Z","created_by":"cc-libc","updated_at":"2026-04-22T13:46:11.806847+00:00","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cdylib","full-review","harness","test-infra"],"assignee":"cc-libc","notes":"Fixed by cc-libc in the same push.

Root cause: scripts/bd15n2_fixture_gap_fill_run.sh maintained a hardcoded LIB_CANDIDATES array of three paths (target/release, target/debug, /data/tmp/cargo-target/release) and never considered a caller-supplied CARGO_TARGET_DIR. Under rch (which redirects to /tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc) or any other non-default target-dir setup, the script's rebuild step put the .so somewhere the candidate list never looked.

Fix: append "${CARGO_TARGET_DIR}/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so" and "${CARGO_TARGET_DIR}/debug/libfrankenlibc_abi.so" to LIB_CANDIDATES when the env var is set, matching the pattern that scripts/e2e_suite.sh already uses correctly.

Extended the same fix to scripts/c_fixture_suite.sh and scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh, which had the identical bug — those are called by sibling gates that would have tripped next.

Also improved the error message to print the searched-path list when the library can't be located, so future breakages are diagnosable at a glance.

Validation: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test bd15n2_fixture_gap_fill_artifacts_test -> 1 passed, 0 failed, 28.96s (worker vmi1152480).","closed_at":"2026-04-22T13:46:11.806847+00:00","close_reason":"done"}

The stderr only shows the cargo 'Finished release' line, no locator stderr from the script — the script likely greps `target/release` for the .so but we're running under `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc` which the script doesn't respect.

That script likely hardcodes `target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so`; change it to honour `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` (or at least look at `/data/tmp/cargo-target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so` when set) or fall back to `cargo metadata --format-version=1 | jq -r .target_directory`/`release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so`.

- Expected to pass under default `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` (unset → defaults to `target/`).
","notes":"Fixed by cc-libc in the same push.

Root cause: scripts/bd15n2_fixture_gap_fill_run.sh maintained a hardcoded LIB_CANDIDATES array of three paths (target/release, target/debug, /data/tmp/cargo-target/release) and never considered a caller-supplied CARGO_TARGET_DIR. Under rch (which redirects to /tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc) or any other non-default target-dir setup, the script's rebuild step put the .so somewhere the candidate list never looked.

Fix: append "${CARGO_TARGET_DIR}/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so" and "${CARGO_TARGET_DIR}/debug/libfrankenlibc_abi.so" to LIB_CANDIDATES when the env var is set, matching the pattern that scripts/e2e_suite.sh already uses correctly.

Extended the same fix to scripts/c_fixture_suite.sh and scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh, which had the identical bug — those are called by sibling gates that would have tripped next.

Also improved the error message to print the searched-path list when the library can't be located, so future breakages are diagnosable at a glance.

Validation: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test bd15n2_fixture_gap_fill_artifacts_test -> 1 passed, 0 failed, 28.96s (worker vmi1152480).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc-libc","created_at":"2026-04-22T13:18:06.994113107Z","created_by":"cc-libc","updated_at":"2026-04-22T13:46:11.806847Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T13:46:11.806847Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cdylib","full-review","harness","test-infra"]}
 {"id":"bd-gjcb5","title":"syscall: pidfd_open current pid should succeed without touching errno","description":"Host glibc returns a live pidfd and leaves errno unchanged for pidfd_open(getpid(), 0). Add focused ABI regression coverage for the self-open success contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberSignal","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:52:47.927908166Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T09:54:59.675969812Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T09:54:59.673097974Z","close_reason":"Locked down host-parity pidfd_open(getpid(), 0) behavior: success with unchanged errno and new ABI regression coverage; verified locally and via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-gkml2","title":"syscall: sched priority bounds for SCHED_IDLE should be zero exactly","description":"Host glibc returns min=0 and max=0 for sched_get_priority_{min,max}(SCHED_IDLE). Add focused ABI regression coverage so the Linux idle-policy bounds contract is frozen exactly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:33:46.282269176Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:42:32.158902160Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T04:42:32.156733204Z","close_reason":"Added sched_priority_bounds_sched_idle_are_zero asserting min=max=0 host-parity.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-glai","title":"io_internal_abi calls libc::tmpfile and libc::fclose directly, violating replacement guard","description":"The `emit_foreign_adoption_via_host_tmpfile` helper in `io_internal_abi.rs` calls `libc::tmpfile()` and `libc::fclose()` directly. This violates the standalone/replacement mode constraints and causes the `check_replacement_guard.sh replacement` check to fail, breaking the build. The test helper should use the native equivalents or be whitelisted.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-18T17:15:42.569092661Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T17:42:32.032312746Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T17:42:32.030364129Z","close_reason":"Exempt conformance-only emit_foreign_adoption_via_host_tmpfile tmpfile/fclose calls from replacement source scan and align guard tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -997,6 +997,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-yaiw","title":"Repair beads SQLite integrity from JSONL source of truth","description":"Repair the local Beads SQLite database after doctor reported a recoverable integrity warning while the JSONL source of truth remained healthy. Current state: issues.jsonl parses with 613 records, br sync status reports the DB and JSONL are in sync with dirty_count 0, but br doctor reports sqlite.integrity_check warning database disk image is malformed page 17 is never used. Recovery plan: snapshot .beads, rebuild an isolated temporary DB from issues.jsonl, verify the temporary DB with br show, br sync --status, and br doctor, then promote it to canonical .beads/beads.db without deleting any historical artifacts.","notes":"Repair summary: snapshot created at .beads/.br_recovery/20260408T032423Z_bd-yaiw. Direct temp rebuild via br sync --import-only --rebuild into .beads/beads.db.rebuild_20260408T032435Z preserved data but still reproduced the SQLite integrity warning. A second-stage VACUUM INTO from that rebuilt DB produced .beads/beads.db.vacuum_20260408T032435Z, which passed SQLite integrity_check, br show, br sync --status, and br doctor. Promotion was non-destructive: moved the prior canonical DB and sidecars to timestamped .bad files, then installed the verified vacuumed DB as .beads/beads.db. Post-promotion state: br doctor reports sqlite.integrity_check ok; remaining warnings are preserved recovery artifacts and a wal-without-shm sidecar expected for frankensqlite.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:21:58.390640706Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-08T03:30:05.440726927Z","closed_at":"2026-04-08T03:30:05.440523393Z","close_reason":"Canonical .beads/beads.db repaired; final post-write vacuum pending to keep integrity_check clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["beads","coordination","infrastructure"]}
 {"id":"bd-yc3o","title":"Conformance: direct execute membrane_mode_split fixture cases","description":"Add direct execute_fixture_case coverage for membrane_mode_split.json so strict-vs-hardened TSM fixture behavior is exercised by the harness test suite.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverHarbor","created_at":"2026-04-18T11:10:39.891094066Z","created_by":"SilverHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-18T11:19:24.333044405Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T11:19:24.331008172Z","close_reason":"Added direct execute_fixture_case coverage for membrane_mode_split fixtures; verified with rch focused test/check/clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","membrane","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-yd0a","title":"Conformance: execute socket_ops fixture cases","description":"Extend the socket_ops harness conformance test so every socket/bind/listen/shutdown/getsockname fixture case runs through frankenlibc_fixture_exec::execute_fixture_case with declared mode expansion and expected_output comparison. Verify with focused rch test/check/clippy.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverHarbor","created_at":"2026-04-18T06:10:51.906672775Z","created_by":"SilverHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-18T06:16:04.844607717Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T06:16:04.842543962Z","close_reason":"Implemented socket_ops executable fixture coverage; verified with rch test/check/clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","socket","testing"]}
+{"id":"bd-ye0f4","title":"[CRITICAL] bd-g2bd5 regression: glibc_internal_abi_test still SIGABRTs on free(): invalid size under parallel test execution","description":"## Regression against bd-g2bd5

bd-g2bd5 was closed at 2026-04-22T14:01:10Z with close_reason 'Scoped __cxa_thread_atexit_impl capture to local TLS dtor tests and revalidated glibc/startup ABI suites' (commit a581f0ac 'br-bd-g2bd5: isolate tls dtor test registrations'). That fix is partial — the heap corruption still reproduces.

## Fresh repro at current HEAD (901b7bcc / 693bb766)

```
$ rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc cargo test --workspace --all-targets
...
test e2e_string_pipeline_strlen_malloc_memcpy_strcmp ... ok
free(): invalid size
error: test failed, to rerun pass `-p frankenlibc-abi --test glibc_internal_abi_test`
  process didn't exit successfully: `.../glibc_internal_abi_test-bcae966c1a14117c` (signal: 6, SIGABRT: process abort signal)
```

Same binary re-run in isolation (`cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test glibc_internal_abi_test`) now appears to pass — so the race has moved from 'reliably reproducing on isolated run' (pre-fix) to 'only reproducing when other workspace tests run concurrently in the same process pool' (post-fix). That's measurable progress, but the heap write onto a freed-or-wrong-size chunk is still live.

## Diagnosis

The a581f0ac fix scoped  capture to its test-local TLS dtor registrations. That closed **one** source of cross-test thread-local leakage. The SIGABRT still firing near  (in a different test binary) suggests either:

1. another set of test TLS/thread-local state is leaking through the same path (e.g. resolv-context override, IDNA allocation pool, wcstof128 static, obstack globals), OR
2. the fix captured TLS dtor registrations correctly but freed its scratch buffer via a path that is inconsistent with the allocation size metadata (e.g. Box::from_raw on a pointer allocated by a different allocator, or layout mismatch on dealloc).

## Fix direction

1. Rerun with MALLOC_CHECK_=2 to get a glibc backtrace from the first write past chunk-size metadata.
2. Rerun under ASan (RUSTFLAGS='-Zsanitizer=address' cargo +nightly test --workspace) — ASan will point at the exact offending free/realloc.
3. Audit remaining thread-local state init/teardown in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/glibc_internal_abi.rs plus any Box::from_raw / custom Drop impl that crosses allocators.

## Provenance / cross-reference

- Previous bead: bd-g2bd5 (closed 2026-04-22T14:01:10Z).
- Previous fix commit: a581f0ac br-bd-g2bd5: isolate tls dtor test registrations.
- Also closed this session: bd-i3om4 (70d3f3ea br-bd-i3om4: serialize runtime policy test state) and bd-nnjiz (901b7bcc br-bd-nnjiz: normalize elimination flake threshold). Both appear to hold under parallel reruns.

## Blast radius

Still blocks cargo test --workspace --all-targets from completing cleanly. --test-threads=1 is a workaround, not a fix.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:21:38.932129482Z","created_by":"cc-libc","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:21:38.932129482Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["abi","critical","full-review","glibc","heap","race","regression","test"]}
 {"id":"bd-yecy","title":"Execute ctype_ops fixture through isolated harness","description":"Upgrade ctype_ops conformance from schema-only validation to per-case isolated harness execution, plus top-level fixture metadata assertions.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-17T00:24:38.190608519Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-17T00:31:16.051680212Z","closed_at":"2026-04-17T00:31:16.048625797Z","close_reason":"Upgraded ctype_ops conformance coverage to execute every fixture case through the isolated harness subprocess path and added top-level fixture metadata assertions.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-yfwo","title":"syscall: inotify_init1 flags veneer","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T08:43:18.318872029Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:47:48.581966989Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T08:47:48.579133696Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -75,6 +75,11 @@ name = "fuzz_socket"
 path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_socket.rs"
 doc = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_fortify"
+path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_fortify.rs"
+doc = false
+
 [[bin]]
 name = "fuzz_resolver"
 path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_resolver.rs"
